creator: jharris@widomaker.com
text:
On Tue, Apr 24, 2007 at 06:48:34AM +0000, Andrew Pantyukhin wrote:

> Synopsis: ports/www/Makefile - add cherokee-devel, sort
> 
> State-Changed-From-To: open->closed
> State-Changed-By: sat
> State-Changed-When: Tue Apr 24 06:43:22 UTC 2007
> State-Changed-Why: 
> Jason, how could you even think of insinuating something like
> me forgetting that :-) Well, the fact is something about
> cherokee came up right after the repocopy and I need to fix
> that before committing the actual port and hooking it up to
> the build.

Sorry.  I know that is always a possibility, but I failed to find
a message specifically mentioning www/cherokee-devel and didn't get
any warnings when I fetched www/cherokee{,-devel}.  I think it would
be clearer/cleaner if the policy were to just mark such ports BROKEN/
IGNORE and still hook them up for INDEX/portsmon/portscout purposes.
Otherwise, not finding something in the INDEX (the logical place to
grep), people may waste time recreating and then finding a "phantom"
port has problems when they could be fixing it instead.

> Anyway,
> cd /usr/ports&&for i in *;do grep SUBDIR $i/Makefile|sort -c;done
> shows that 20 other Makefiles are disordered. Care to provide more
> patches?

I checked a few and found disorder, but didn't want to do submit a
patch for www/Makefile that wasn't technically correct or submit a
mega-patch that hid the addition of one line among thousands.  But,
OK, I'll mention in my next PR that you'd like to handle it.  :)

> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=112052
> This one has been committed, thanks!

Thanks.

--
